name: EOA – Blotato Social Posting Pipeline
description: Triggered by the video clip pipeline (Lincoln) or manually. Generates platform-specific captions via AI, then schedules posts across Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, and Twitter via the EOA social posting API (which calls Blotato).

nodes.name: Webhook – Video Clip Ready
nodes.notes: Call this webhook from Lincoln (video pipeline) when a clip is ready, or from any manual trigger. Expected payload: { videoUrl, transcript, eventTitle, eventId, scheduledAt (ISO8601, optional), platforms (optional array) }

nodes.name: AI – Generate Platform Captions
nodes.notes: Uses OpenAI to generate all 4 platform captions from the event transcript. Replace with your preferred LLM if needed.

nodes.name: Notify – Telegram

parameters.name: text

nodes.notes: Optional — notify the EOA ops Telegram group when posts are scheduled. Remove if not needed.

notes:
ENV VARS REQUIRED (set in N8N environment):
  EOA_SITE_URL       — e.g. https://eoa.cm or http://localhost:3000 for local dev
  ADMIN_SECRET       — must match ADMIN_SECRET in Next.js .env.local
  OPENAI_API_KEY     — for the AI caption generation node
  TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN — for completion notifications (optional)
  TELEGRAM_GROUP_ID  — EOA ops group ID (optional)

BLOTATO SETUP (in Next.js .env.local):
  BLOTATO_API_KEY                — from Blotato Settings → API Keys
  BLOTATO_ACCOUNT_ID_FACEBOOK    — from Blotato Settings → Connected Accounts
  BLOTATO_ACCOUNT_ID_INSTAGRAM   — from Blotato Settings → Connected Accounts
  BLOTATO_ACCOUNT_ID_LINKEDIN    — from Blotato Settings → Connected Accounts
  BLOTATO_ACCOUNT_ID_TWITTER     — from Blotato Settings → Connected Accounts

INTEGRATION WITH VIDEO PIPELINE (Lincoln):
  After Lincoln generates a video clip, call this webhook:
  POST https://your-n8n.com/webhook/eoa-social-post
  Body: { videoUrl, transcript, eventTitle, eventId, scheduledAt? }
